title: Bill Simmons seems convinced that ‘all signs are pointing to’ this date for Jayson Tatum’s return
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Bill Simmons suggests Jayson Tatum may return Friday against the Mavericks at TD Garden.
Tatum cleared for practice but remains out due to Achilles tendon repair for Tuesday's game against Bucks.
The Celtics have not confirmed Tatum's return date; team is 40-20 without him this season.

title: Should teens be allowed to carry handguns in Massachusetts?
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: The Massachusetts Supreme Judicial Court will consider if the law barring under-21s from carrying handguns is constitutional.
The case arose from Mikai Thomson's arrest; he claims the age limit violates his Second Amendment rights.
A decision against Massachusetts could allow 18- to 20-year-olds to legally carry handguns in the state.

title: 5 takeaways from Don Sweeney’s Bruins press conference before trade deadline
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Don Sweeney, GM of the Bruins, aims to enhance the roster without compromising long-term goals before Friday's trade deadline.
Bruins plan to avoid short-term rentals, focusing instead on acquiring players with long-term contracts who can be 'difference-makers'.
Despite past aggressive trades, Sweeney maintains no regrets about previous roster moves aimed at immediate success.

title: Giannis Antetokounmpo set to return against Celtics while Jaylen Brown is out with an illness
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Giannis Antetokounmpo is set to return against the Celtics after missing 15 games due to a right calf strain.
Celtics' Jaylen Brown will miss the game against the Bucks due to a non-COVID illness, and Neemias Queta will rest.
The Celtics are currently second in the Eastern Conference, while the Bucks are in 11th place, chasing a play-in spot.

title: Citing ‘wokeness,’ Pentagon cancels military fellowships at New England schools like MIT and Tufts
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: The Pentagon canceled military fellowships at MIT, Tufts, Brown, and other institutions due to alleged 'leftist indoctrination.'
Secretary of Defense Pete Hegseth announced 93 fellowships will be cut starting with the 2026-27 academic year.
New potential fellowship partners include state schools like the University of Michigan, focusing on 'intellectual freedom.'

title: Rescued pocket-sized owl turning heads in Mass.
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: A northern saw-whet owl was found injured in a New England driveway and is currently under care at Newhouse Wildlife Rescue.
The owl, about 6.7 to 8.3 inches tall, has two broken bones and is receiving treatment at Cape Ann Wildlife.
An online fundraiser supports Cape Ann Wildlife, whose founder is battling lung cancer, as they care for the injured owl.

title: Massachusetts joins insurers cutting coverage for popular weight-loss drugs
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Massachusetts Group Insurance Commission voted 10-7 to end GLP-1 drug coverage for state employees due to budget constraints.
The policy change affects 22,000 users and aims to reduce state drug spending by approximately $100 million.
Opponents argue the decision might increase future health costs and disproportionately impact lower-income members.

title: After Columbia incident, Healey and Campbell remind schools that ICE needs a judicial warrant to enter dorms
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Massachusetts officials Gov. Maura Healey and AG Andrea Campbell reminded schools ICE needs a judicial warrant to enter dorms.
ICE agents reportedly entered a Columbia building falsely claiming to search for a missing child, targeting student Elmina Aghayeva.
Officials issued guidance to educational institutions on rights regarding federal agents; aims to protect students and staff.

title: Lawsuit challenges National Park Service over content removal, including at a Mass. site
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: A lawsuit filed in Boston claims the Trump administration's order forces national parks to censor historical content.
Lowell National Historical Park halted films about labor history due to compliance with a Trump executive order.
Acadia National Park removed signs on climate change impacts, prompting a lawsuit that resulted in restoration.

title: Mass. police dispatcher injured in Iran conflict
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: A Sudbury police dispatcher in the Navy Reserve was injured by missile strikes while stationed in Dubai.
The dispatcher, seeking anonymity, suffered minor injuries from Iranian missiles that landed near their base.
The U.S.-Israeli strikes in Iran have faced criticism from Massachusetts lawmakers as illegal and potentially escalating conflict.
